Tutorial - GMS2 Top Down Shooter

If you want to see tutorial in a separate web browser window click here.

Setting up Three Enemy Types

We need to now import our three enemy planes that we will be combatting. We will do this by importing strips like we did for the player. We use this method when all frames are tiled on one sheet.

The enemy sprites have 2 frames and they are 64 x 64 pixels. We will import them as a sprite sheet (or strip). Create a new Sprite by clickng on Create Sprite by right clicking on the Sprite title in the Resources folder on the right hand side. The press the Edit Image button. Click the Image menu item on the top and select Import strip image. Go to Included Files | TutorialResources | Sprites | spr_enemy_basic_sprite_sheet.png . If you can't find it you can locate the file in the browser (or click here).

Name the new sprite: spr_enemy_basic. Basic stands for an enemy that doesn't shoot. It just flies and has no projectile weapons.

Repeat this by doing the same for spr_enemy_shoot_sprite_sheet and spr_enemy_target_sprite_sheet. Import these sprite strips and call them spr_enemy_shoot and spr_enemy_target

Make sure the origin is set to Middle Center on all three enemy sprites:

Now we will use another parent object to hold our common behavior as well as act as our collision object that we will be checking when firing bullets. Create a new Game Object and call it obj_enemy_parent.

Now create three new Game Objects: obj_enemy_basic, obj_enemy_shoot and obj_enemy_target with the correct sprites and inherit the parent obj_enemy_parent:
